claim: The proportion of people with visual difficulty is two times higher in high-income countries than in low-income countries.

result.claim: The proportion of people with visual difficulty is two times higher in high-income countries than in low-income countries.
result.reasoning: The claim states that the proportion of people with visual difficulty is two times higher in high-income countries than in low-income countries. However, evidence from "The Lancet Global Health Commission on Global Eye Health: vision beyond 2020" (Paper ID: W3130938027), a review paper with a credibility rating of 3.0/5 and 1291 citations, directly refutes this. Propositions from this paper (prop_202211 and prop_202244) explicitly state that "90% of those affected by vision impairment live in low-income and middle-income countries (LMICs)" and "Most people affected by vision impairment live in low-income and middle-income countries (LMICs)." The abstract of the paper further confirms this, stating, "A large proportion of those affected (90%), live in low-income and middle-income countries (LMICs)." This indicates that the prevalence of visual difficulty is significantly higher in low-income and middle-income countries, not high-income countries.
